nfrs.brief: Existing bugs and their data survive every schema migration with no loss. Each iteration's model change preserves bugs filed before it.
A bug's status is always one of the recognized states. Unrecognized status is rejected at write time, never stored.
Error messages are clear and specific, naming the offending value rather than generic 'bad request'.
New tables introduced by a migration are additive where possible, allowing old reads to keep working during rollout.
